expectations.expectation_text: The output includes a <role> block identifying it as an orchestrator
expectations.text: Role block is clearly defined and identifies the agent as a specialized CI/CD Pipeline Orchestrator.
expectations.evidence: System prompt begins with: '<role>You are the CI/CD Pipeline Orchestrator Agent — a specialized coordinator for software delivery quality gates. Your mission is to orchestrate three sibling agents (security scanner, unit test runner, and code quality checker) in parallel, aggregate their verdicts, and return a single authoritative PASS/FAIL verdict with a consolidated quality report.</role>' The role explicitly states the orchestration mission and establishes the agent's position in the pipeline.

expectations.expectation_text: The output describes coordination of multiple sub-agents or pipeline stages
expectations.text: The system prompt extensively documents the orchestration of three sub-agents and multi-stage coordination.
expectations.evidence: Section '### Sub-Agent Invocation Contract' specifies invocation schema for three agents: security_scanner, unit_test_runner, code_quality_checker. Section '### Parallel Execution & Timeout Handling' documents coordination: 'Invoke all three sub-agents in parallel. Set a global pipeline timeout of 300 seconds (5 minutes). Individual agent timeouts are 250 seconds.' The example demonstrates real-world invocation of all three agents with aggregated results.

expectations.expectation_text: The output includes a JSON schema for the consolidated report
expectations.text: Complete JSON schema defined with consolidated_report structure and all required fields.
expectations.evidence: Section '<output_format>' defines complete JSON structure including 'consolidated_report' object with: decision, decision_rationale, risk_assessment, recommendations, manual_review_required, timeout_agents, failed_agents. The schema uses strict typing with enums (PASS|FAIL|REVIEW, LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH). All fields are machine-parseable with no 'any' types.

expectations.expectation_text: The output includes PASS/FAIL or similar verdict logic
expectations.text: Detailed decision logic table and verdict rules are fully specified with explicit thresholds.
expectations.evidence: Section '### Decision Logic & Thresholds' provides explicit rules: '**Final Verdict Rule:** PASS: All three sub-agents return PASS verdict AND all numeric thresholds met. REVIEW: At least one sub-agent returns REVIEW OR at least one threshold is at the boundary. FAIL: Any sub-agent returns FAIL OR any critical threshold violated.' Includes threshold table with Signal, PASS Threshold, REVIEW Threshold, and FAIL Threshold columns for all three sub-agents.

expectations.expectation_text: The output includes a <failure_modes> section covering sub-agent failure
expectations.text: Comprehensive failure_modes section with 7 distinct failure scenarios.
expectations.evidence: Section '<failure_modes>' documents: (1) Required Input Missing (returns FAIL), (2) Sub-Agent Timeout >250s (marks as REVIEW, continues), (3) Invalid JSON/Error Code (marks as REVIEW, continues), (4) Low Confidence (verdict REVIEW, manual review required), (5) Out-of-Scope Request (FAIL), (6) All Three Sub-Agents Timeout (REVIEW), (7) Inconsistent Agent Versions (normalization attempt, else REVIEW). Each scenario includes Condition, Action, and rationale.

expectations.text: Hard constraints section with 12 explicit rules governing orchestrator behavior.
expectations.evidence: Section '<constraints>' defines 12 hard rules: (1) No Agent Invocation Side Effects, (2) Parallel Agent Invocation Only, (3) Timeout is 250 seconds per sub-agent, (4) No Agent Verdict Override, (5) Mandatory JSON Schema Compliance, (6) No Partial Results, (7) Exit Code Integrity, (8) Agent-Studio Tech Stack Adherence, (9) No Secrets in Logs, (10) Deterministic Verdict Logic, (11) No Implicit Dependencies, (12) Standard Compliance. Each rule includes justification and scope.

expectations.expectation_text: The output includes an <example> block showing sample input/output
expectations.text: Realistic end-to-end example with concrete input and complete JSON output.
expectations.evidence: Section '<example>' titled 'Real-World CI/CD Pipeline Run' includes: Input (repository_path, commit_hash, branch='feature/ecc-integration', scope='diff-only'). Output shows all three sub-agent results (security_scanner score 95, unit_test_runner score 98 with 2880/2880 tests passed, code_quality_checker score 88). Consolidated report shows decision='PASS', risk_assessment='LOW', and metrics showing 287ms orchestration duration with all agents completed. Example demonstrates practical usage with business context.

expectations.expectation_text: The total system prompt is at least 4000 characters
expectations.text: System prompt substantially exceeds the 4000 character minimum.
expectations.evidence: The metrics.json file reports: 'System prompt: 8,247 characters' and 'Minimum 4000 characters | ✓ | 2/2 | Total content: 8,247 characters (system prompt + appendix)'. The character count is more than double the minimum requirement, with comprehensive coverage across all sections: role (174 chars), methodology (2,100 chars), decision logic (800 chars), output format (1,900 chars), failure modes (1,500 chars), example (2,100 chars), constraints (1,200 chars), and appendix (450 chars).

preconditions: Sub-agents registered in agent roster: security_scanner, unit_test_runner, code_quality_checker
Sub-agent invocation endpoints documented (REST API or MCP)
CI/CD system configured to call orchestrator with valid repository_path, commit_hash, branch
Monitoring/observability pipeline configured for orchestration_duration_ms, agents_timed_out, failed_agents
recommended_next_steps: Register the three sub-agents in agent roster
Document sub-agent invocation endpoints (REST API or MCP)
Set up CI/CD system to call orchestrator with valid parameters
Monitor orchestration duration and sub-agent response times in first week
Establish baseline for verdict distribution (target: 85% PASS, 10% REVIEW, 5% FAIL)

strengths: Comprehensive parallel orchestration model with explicit timeout handling (300s global, 250s per agent)
Graceful degradation strategy—sub-agent failures mark as REVIEW and continue (no cascading failures)
Domain-specific constraints aligned with agent-studio tech stack (Next.js 15.5, TypeScript strict, Railway PostgreSQL, OWASP 2025, CVSS v4.0)
Deterministic decision logic with explicit threshold table and clear verdict rules
Realistic example demonstrating end-to-end pipeline with 2880 test count, 89.2% coverage metrics
Extensive failure mode coverage (7 scenarios with condition→action mappings)
Schema normalization appendix handles non-standard sub-agent response formats
No side-effects constraint prevents accidental code/DB mutations
Clear metrics and observability fields (duration_ms, agents_completed, agents_timed_out)
areas_for_future_improvement: Agent discovery mechanism could document how orchestrator validates sub-agent endpoints (currently assumes pre-registration)
Extension pathway for adding 4th agent (e.g., performance benchmarking) is documented but implementation template not provided
Sub-agent versioning strategy beyond schema normalization could be more detailed
Cost analysis not included (e.g., OpenAI embeddings cost per pipeline run) — could be added to metrics
grader_notes: This agent orchestrator system prompt is production-grade and meets all 8 expectations with high quality across all dimensions. The output demonstrates mastery of: (1) contract-first design (Anthropic 2026 standards), (2) multi-agent coordination patterns, (3) failure mode handling with graceful degradation, (4) domain-specific knowledge (agent-studio stack), and (5) comprehensive documentation. The system prompt is ready for immediate deployment to a real CI/CD pipeline. Rubric: 20/20. Recommendation: PASS with EXCELLENCE.
